objective: Independently review the complete HCM-0.1 Handbook Contract Membrane control pack against live repo truth and close the docs-only proof gate only if no actionable finding remains.

sections: Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections

work_completed: Revalidated the source handoff against the current branch, HEAD, dirty paths, exact live Rust/assets, and the absence of target artifact/intake/posture/snapshot Rust types.

Reviewed kind/instance ownership, custom-kind safety, shared-schema intake modes, provenance/candidate/canonical authority, Charter projections, posture recommendation/transition authority, Snapshot integration, default-set reservation, phase sequencing, orchestration, and proof gates.

Validated current handoff records and ledger entries with Draft 2020-12 schemas, checked record/index uniqueness and parity, checked relative Markdown targets, checked active control-pack portability, and ran git diff checks.
Kept the review docs-only and did not modify the reviewed control pack, Rust, tests, assets, or installed skills.

deliverables.summary: Immutable fresh independent review record with findings-first evidence, bounded remediation routes, and an HCM-0.1 open verdict.

decisions.summary: The source handoff's core live-repo and target-only implementation claims remain accurate after current-tree verification, but its required independent proof follow-up does not close cleanly.
decisions.authority_effect: The existing kind/intake/Charter/posture/Snapshot/default-set semantic direction remains review input, while HCM-0.1 stays open for bounded documentation repair and a later fresh review.

decisions.summary: The HCM-0.1 independent review is not clean because actionable control-pack and handoff-protocol findings remain.
decisions.authority_effect: The HCM-0.1 control-pack proof gate is not satisfied; no runtime seam promotion or Rust authorization is earned.

findings.summary: The HCM-0.1 exit row requires the pack to be reviewed, and the corrected dispatch requires a clean review, but the authoritative Control-pack proof gate requires only that independent review be requested. A request can satisfy the written gate before any result exists. Bounded repair: require a completed independent review with no unresolved actionable findings, and require remediation plus a later fresh review after any finding.

Phase 2 authorizes Resolution-specific Charter views and a deterministic configured custom-kind projection before Phase 3 implements the Context Resolution kernel and deterministic projection engine. The pack does not distinguish a safe pre-Resolution renderer from the later generic engine. Bounded repair decision: either limit Phase 2 to fixed deterministic review renderers and defer Resolution-aware/configured projection proof, or define an explicit earlier declarative projection primitive with ownership, safety, proof, and replacement boundaries.

The canonical handoff closeout procedure labels jq empty as record validation and never validates the generated record or ledger entry against the published Draft 2020-12 schemas. Structurally invalid durable records can therefore pass the documented closeout. Bounded repair: add a repository-owned Draft 2020-12 validation command/script for both artifacts and retain jq only as a syntax precheck.

The protocol requires every new handoff to carry snapshot_refs and applicable semantic_refs, but the v1.0 schema's top-level required list omits both properties, so schema-valid new records may silently violate the protocol. Backward compatibility with immutable older records needs an explicit versioned schema strategy rather than leaving new-record requirements unenforced.

The SnapshotCapturePolicy example defines bounded recent-completed and queued-next windows without cursors, while its gate requires every bounded history window to name count, source, cursor, and ordering. Bounded repair: add explicit cursor fields, including an intentional null/initial cursor rule if appropriate, or revise the gate consistently.

The schema policy requires every structured record to carry stable schema_id and version, but the ArtifactInstanceDescriptor contract example contains neither. Bounded repair: add the descriptor schema identity/version fields or explicitly narrow the policy and define where descriptor versioning lives.

blockers.summary: HCM-0.1 cannot close while the six actionable review findings remain in the authoritative control pack and handoff protocol.
blockers.resume_condition: A separate docs-only remediation session resolves the findings and a later fresh independent reviewer returns no actionable findings.

escalations.trigger: Phase 2 requires Resolution-specific and generic configured projections before the Phase 3 Resolution kernel and deterministic projection engine exist.
escalations.target_resolution: Phase 0 architecture/design authority for the pre-Resolution renderer versus general projection-engine boundary
escalations.decision_required: Choose whether Phase 2 is limited to fixed deterministic review rendering or receives an explicitly defined safe declarative projection primitive before Phase 3.
options: Limit Phase 2 to fixed deterministic non-Resolution review renderers and move Resolution-aware/configured custom projection proof after HCM-3.2/HCM-3.3.
Define and sequence a bounded declarative projection primitive before Phase 2 proof, with safe resolution, ownership, proof gates, and an explicit relationship to HCM-3.3.
escalations.recommendation: Prefer the first option to preserve the stated semantic-authority-to-projection sequence and avoid creating an undocumented precursor engine.
escalations.resume_condition: The chosen boundary is recorded consistently in architecture, semantic, phase, contract, and proof sections, then independently re-reviewed.

validation.command: Live branch/HEAD/dirty-state inspection plus exact Rust/assets review and target-type absence scan

Absolute-machine-path scan across 00-08 control-pack files and handoff schema/template infrastructure

sections: Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections
pack_updates.reason: Resolve the Phase 2 versus Phase 3 projection-engine sequencing and authorization boundary.
Align structured-record identity, pre-Resolution projection semantics, and deterministic bounded-window cursor requirements.
Require a completed clean independent review rather than a request for review.
Define real Draft 2020-12 record/ledger validation and a versioned new-record requirement for snapshot and semantic refs.
Enforce new-record protocol requirements without invalidating immutable older records, using an explicit schema-version strategy.

next_session.objective: Revalidate these findings and dispatch one bounded docs-only HCM-0.1 remediation session; after remediation, dispatch a different fresh independent reviewer for the complete control-pack gate.

sections: Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections

allowed_scope: A bounded docs-only remediation of the exact findings in HCM-R-F-001 through HCM-R-F-006.
Draft 2020-12 validation tooling or documented repository-owned validation commands needed to enforce the handoff/ledger schemas.
A later fresh independent docs-only review after remediation.
prohibited_scope: Rust, test, core asset, or installed-skill implementation.
Selecting or implying the shipped default artifact set.
Starting HCM-0.2, HCM-0.3, HCM-0.6, HCM-0.7, or any implementation slice.
Mutating this immutable review record or the reviewed orchestration records.
acceptance_criteria: The control-pack gate requires a completed clean independent review.
Phase 2 and Phase 3 projection responsibilities are sequenced without hidden implementation authority.
Record and ledger closeout uses actual Draft 2020-12 schema validation.
New handoff snapshot/semantic-ref requirements are enforced through an explicit version-compatible schema strategy.
Snapshot bounded windows and artifact-instance schema identity are internally consistent.
A later fresh reviewer reports no actionable findings before HCM-0.1 closes.
stop_conditions: Stop if remediation would choose shipped defaults or authorize Rust.
Stop if the projection boundary needs broader product authority than the bounded Phase 0 architecture decision.
Do not self-review the remediation; dispatch a different fresh reviewer.

chat_summary: Fresh independent review found six actionable control-pack/protocol issues, including a review-request-only close gate, Phase 2/3 projection sequencing ambiguity, and schema-enforcement gaps. HCM-0.1 remains open; no Rust or reviewed-pack files changed.
